weight_transform: cos/sin are precomputed per-position matrices broadcast over heads (cos_matrix/sin_matrix, shape [1, 1|B, S, head_dim], TILE_LAYOUT, replicated across mesh). transformation_mat = get_rot_transformation_mat(): a static 32x32-tile permutation matrix that implements rotate_half() as a matmul on-device; built once per mode ('decode'/'prefill') and reused. Inputs must be bfloat16 (call sites insert ttnn.typecast to bfloat16 before the op). Some models pre-permute Q/K projection rows at init so head-dim is already in interleaved (even/odd) layout, avoiding per-call reshape/permute. If head_dim is not tile-padded, cos is padded with 1.0 and sin with 0.0 (pad_head_dim).
applicability_notes: fusion_notes: Q and K RoPE appear as two sibling calls sharing the same cos/sin/trans_mat; a fused_qk decode variant exists (to_qk_fused_memory_config + fused path). Frequently chained: typecast(bf16) -> rotary_embedding_llama -> typecast(bf8_b) -> SDPA, or permute/to_memory_config(shard) -> rope -> to_memory_config(L1 interleaved) -> permute in decode.

placement_observations.size_descriptor: decode token: [1, batch, n_heads, head_dim] e.g. [1, 32, 16, 64]
placement_observations.source: deepseek q_rope/kv_rope decode call sites ('1,32,16,64 height sharded 8x4 [32,64]'), perf test ('HEIGHT_SHARDED with 32 cores'); inputs resharded via to_memory_config(rope_sharded_mem_config) before the op

memory_config.shard_spec_template: [32, {HEAD_DIM}] on 8x4 grid; immediately resharded to L1 interleaved via ttnn.to_memory_config(..., ttnn.L1_MEMORY_CONFIG)

placement_observations.source: deepseek decode call site comments ('# 1,32,16,64 width sharded 8x4 [32,64]' then to_memory_config L1 interleaved)
call site comment: 'Use is_decode_mode=False to work with DRAM layout for all sequence lengths'; llama prefill q_heads_1QSD/k_heads_1KSD paths
call sites passing memory_config=ttnn.L1_MEMORY_CONFIG explicitly with is_decode_mode=False and compute_kernel_config=self.rope_compute_kernel_config / self.kcfg
rope_setup.get_rot_mats / rope_tensors['cos_matrix','sin_matrix'] created once and reused across Q and K calls; mesh tests use ReplicateTensorToMesh
